subject: Gaming
title: Virtual Economies: Real Money in Fake Worlds
description: A 60-second lesson on Virtual Economies: Real Money in Fake Worlds.

hook.text: A virtual sword sold for $1.6 million. Virtual economies now rival small countries in total value.

buildup.text: MMOs created economies with supply, demand, inflation, and trade. Economists study them as real systems.

discovery.text: EVE Online hired a real economist to manage its virtual economy. Player actions caused market crashes.

twist.text: Gold farming became real employment. Workers in developing nations grind games for virtual currency sold to Western players.

climax.text: Virtual economies blurred the line between play and work. Digital items have real monetary value.

punchline.text: A virtual sword worth millions. The economy is fake. The money is real.

quiz.question: Why did EVE Online hire a real economist?
options: To manage its virtual economy where player actions caused market crashes
To set the game's subscription price
